name: HRA Calculator
metaTitle: HRA Calculator — How Much House Rent Allowance Is Tax-Free? | c7xai
metaDescription: Calculate your HRA exemption under Section 10(13A). See exactly how much of your HRA is tax-free. Metro/non-metro, rent vs 10% basic. Client-side, no PII.

faq.question: How is HRA exemption calculated?
faq.answer: HRA exemption under Section 10(13A) is the minimum of: (1) Actual HRA received, (2) Rent paid minus 10% of basic salary, (3) 50% of basic salary if in a metro city or 40% if non-metro. Only the lowest of these three is exempt from tax.

faq.question: Can I claim HRA if I don't live in a metro city?
faq.answer: Yes, but the limit is 40% of basic salary instead of 50%. Metro cities for HRA purposes are Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Chennai. All other cities including Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Pune are non-metro.

faq.question: What if my rent is less than 10% of basic salary?
faq.answer: If rent paid is less than 10% of your basic salary, you get zero HRA exemption. The formula requires (Rent - 10% of Basic) to be positive. This means if your basic is ₹50,000 and rent is ₹4,000, you get no exemption since ₹4,000 < ₹5,000 (10% of basic).

faq.question: Can I claim HRA and home loan interest both?
faq.answer: Yes, under specific conditions. If you own a house with a home loan and also pay rent (e.g., you work in a different city), you can claim both HRA exemption and home loan interest deduction under Section 24(b).
